# LLM Greeting Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Replace `ChatPanel`'s hardcoded opener (greeting + 3 suggestion chips) with per-slug values generated by `/fit` at build time and baked into `data/adapted/{slug}.json`.

**Architecture:** `/fit` emits `greeting` and `suggestions` in the fit-summary HTML comment at the top of `data/fitted/{slug}.md`. `/structurize` copies them through to `meta.agentfolio.{greeting,suggestions}` in `data/adapted/{slug}.json`. `App.tsx` forwards these to `ChatPanel` as props. Hardcoded values remain as fallbacks when the JSON meta is absent or malformed.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, React, Vitest. Skills in `.claude/skills/` are Markdown prompts executed by Claude. No new runtime deps.

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-llm-greeting-design.md`

---

## Files

**Modify:**
- `web/src/utils/parseFitSummary.ts` — extend type + parser to handle new fields; fix bullet-to-section attribution
- `web/src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts` — add cases for greeting/suggestions/backward-compat
- `web/src/types.ts:80-88` — extend `AgentfolioMeta` with `greeting?` and `suggestions?`
- `web/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx` — add props `greeting?` / `suggestions?` with fallback logic
- `web/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx` — add cases for prop-provided greeting + chips
- `web/src/App.tsx:64-72` — read `adapted.meta?.agentfolio?.{greeting,suggestions}` and forward
- `.claude/skills/fit.md` — instruct the skill to emit two new fit-summary fields with constraints
- `.claude/skills/structurize.md` — instruct the skill to copy the two new fields into `meta.agentfolio`
- `data/fitted/default.md`, `data/fitted/notion.md` — regenerated by `/fit` (or hand-edited)
- `data/adapted/default.json`, `data/adapted/notion.json` — regenerated by `/structurize`

**Not touched:** `proxy/src/prompt.ts` (its fit-summary regex only reads `target:`, unaffected), `web/scripts/copy-data.cjs` (only reads `target:`, unaffected), `Dashboard.tsx` and `FittedPreview.tsx` (still use `target`/`changes`, unaffected by the additive parser change).

---

## Task 1: Extend `parseFitSummary` to handle `greeting` + `suggestions`

**Why first:** the existing parser collects every bulleted line into `changes`. Once `suggestions:` (also bulleted) enters the block, suggestion bullets would be misassigned to `changes`. The parser must distinguish sections before any fit-summary containing the new fields enters the repo.

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/utils/parseFitSummary.ts`
- Test: `web/src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests**

Open `web/src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts`. Keep the existing two tests untouched. Append the following block inside the `describe('parseFitSummary', …)` block (or add a second `describe` below it — either works):

```ts
const withGreetingAndSuggestions = `<!--
fit-summary:
  target: Notion — Software Engineer, Enterprise Data Platform
  changes:
    - Changed headline to emphasize data infrastructure
    - Reordered Skills above Projects
  greeting: Hey — I'm an agent that knows Alex. Ask me about the Flink pipeline at Acme or what drew me to Notion's data platform.
  suggestions:
    - Why Notion?
    - Walk me through the Flink pipeline
    - What's not on the résumé?
-->
# Alex Chen
`;

it('parses greeting when present', () => {
  const { summary } = parseFitSummary(withGreetingAndSuggestions);
  expect(summary).not.toBeNull();
  expect(summary!.greeting).toBe(
    "Hey — I'm an agent that knows Alex. Ask me about the Flink pipeline at Acme or what drew me to Notion's data platform.",
  );
});

it('parses suggestions as a separate list, not mixed into changes', () => {
  const { summary } = parseFitSummary(withGreetingAndSuggestions);
  expect(summary!.suggestions).toEqual([
    'Why Notion?',
    'Walk me through the Flink pipeline',
    "What's not on the résumé?",
  ]);
  expect(summary!.changes).toEqual([
    'Changed headline to emphasize data infrastructure',
    'Reordered Skills above Projects',
  ]);
});

it('leaves greeting and suggestions undefined when absent (backward compat)', () => {
  const { summary } = parseFitSummary(withSummary);
  expect(summary!.greeting).toBeUndefined();
  expect(summary!.suggestions).toBeUndefined();
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run tests to verify they fail**

Run: `cd web && npx vitest run src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts`
Expected: the three new tests fail (greeting is undefined / suggestions is undefined / suggestions leaked into changes). Existing two tests still pass.

- [ ] **Step 3: Replace the parser implementation**

Replace the entire contents of `web/src/utils/parseFitSummary.ts` with:

```ts
export interface FitSummary {
  target: string;
  changes: string[];
  greeting?: string;
  suggestions?: string[];
}

const COMMENT_RE = /^<!--\s*\nfit-summary:\s*\n([\s\S]*?)-->\s*\n/;
const LIST_HEADER_RE = /^(\w+):\s*$/;        // `changes:` or `suggestions:`
const SCALAR_RE = /^(\w+):\s+(.+)$/;         // `target: value` or `greeting: value`
const BULLET_RE = /^\s+-\s+(.+)$/;           // `  - item`

export function parseFitSummary(markdown: string): { summary: FitSummary | null; body: string } {
  const match = markdown.match(COMMENT_RE);
  if (!match) return { summary: null, body: markdown };

  const block = match[1];
  let target = '';
  let greeting: string | undefined;
  const changes: string[] = [];
  const suggestions: string[] = [];
  let currentList: string[] | null = null;

  for (const line of block.split('\n')) {
    const bullet = line.match(BULLET_RE);
    if (bullet && currentList) {
      currentList.push(bullet[1].trim());
      continue;
    }

    const listHeader = line.match(LIST_HEADER_RE);
    if (listHeader) {
      if (listHeader[1] === 'changes') currentList = changes;
      else if (listHeader[1] === 'suggestions') currentList = suggestions;
      else currentList = null;
      continue;
    }

    const scalar = line.match(SCALAR_RE);
    if (scalar) {
      if (scalar[1] === 'target') target = scalar[2].trim();
      else if (scalar[1] === 'greeting') greeting = scalar[2].trim();
      currentList = null;
    }
  }

  const summary: FitSummary | null =
    target && changes.length > 0
      ? {
          target,
          changes,
          greeting: greeting || undefined,
          suggestions: suggestions.length > 0 ? suggestions : undefined,
        }
      : null;

  return { summary, body: markdown.slice(match[0].length) };
}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Run tests to verify they pass**

Run: `cd web && npx vitest run src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts`
Expected: all 5 tests pass (2 original + 3 new).

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the full unit suite to ensure no regression**

Run: `cd web && npx vitest run`
Expected: all existing tests still pass. `FittedPreview` and `FittedDiff` continue to render `target`/`changes` unchanged.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add web/src/utils/parseFitSummary.ts web/src/__tests__/parseFitSummary.test.ts
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: extend parseFitSummary to handle greeting + suggestions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Task 2: Extend `AgentfolioMeta` type and wire `ChatPanel` props

**Files:**
- Modify: `web/src/types.ts:80-88`
- Modify: `web/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx`
- Test: `web/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Extend the type**

In `web/src/types.ts`, update the `AgentfolioMeta` interface (currently lines 80–88) to add two optional fields:

```ts
export interface AgentfolioMeta {
  company?: string;
  generated_by?: string;
  summary_template?: string;
  summary_defaults?: Record<string, string>;
  match_score?: MatchScore;
  skill_emphasis?: string[];
  section_order?: SectionName[];
  greeting?: string;
  suggestions?: string[];
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write failing tests**

In `web/src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx`, inside the existing `describe('ChatPanel — inline default state', …)` block, append:

```tsx
it('renders the greeting prop when provided, replacing the hardcoded line', () => {
  vi.stubEnv('VITE_CHAT_PROXY_URL', 'https://proxy.example');
  render(
    <ChatPanel
      slug="notion"
      ownerName="Alex Chen"
      greeting="Hey — I'm an agent that knows Alex. Ask me about the Flink pipeline."
    />,
  );
  const greeting = screen.getByTestId('chat-greeting');
  expect(greeting).toHaveTextContent(
    /Hey — I'm an agent that knows Alex\. Ask me about the Flink pipeline\./,
  );
  expect(greeting).not.toHaveTextContent(/Ask me anything/);
});

it('renders three tailored suggestion chips when suggestions prop is provided', () => {
  vi.stubEnv('VITE_CHAT_PROXY_URL', 'https://proxy.example');
  render(
    <ChatPanel
      slug="notion"
      ownerName="Alex Chen"
      suggestions={['Why Notion?', 'Walk me through the Flink pipeline', "What's not on the résumé?"]}
    />,
  );
  const chips = screen.getAllByTestId('chat-suggestion').map((el) => el.textContent);
  expect(chips).toEqual([
    'Why Notion?',
    'Walk me through the Flink pipeline',
    "What's not on the résumé?",
  ]);
});

it('falls back to DEFAULT_SUGGESTIONS when suggestions prop is not an array of exactly 3', () => {
  vi.stubEnv('VITE_CHAT_PROXY_URL', 'https://proxy.example');
  render(
    <ChatPanel
      slug="notion"
      ownerName="Alex Chen"
      suggestions={['only one']}
    />,
  );
  const chips = screen.getAllByTestId('chat-suggestion').map((el) => el.textContent);
  expect(chips).toEqual([
    'Walk me through the résumé',
    'Why is this a fit?',
    "What's not on the résumé?",
  ]);
});

it('falls back to the hardcoded greeting when greeting prop is empty', () => {
  vi.stubEnv('VITE_CHAT_PROXY_URL', 'https://proxy.example');
  render(<ChatPanel slug="notion" ownerName="Alex Chen" greeting="" />);
  const greeting = screen.getByTestId('chat-greeting');
  expect(greeting).toHaveTextContent(
    /Hey, I'm an agent that knows Alex Chen\. Ask me anything\./,
  );
});
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Run tests to verify they fail**

Run: `cd web && npx vitest run src/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx`
Expected: the four new tests fail (TypeScript may also flag unknown props — that's expected before Step 4). Existing tests still pass.

- [ ] **Step 4: Update `ChatPanel.tsx`**

Apply two edits in `web/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx`:

**(a)** Update the `ChatPanelProps` interface (lines 8–14) to add two optional props:

```ts
export interface ChatPanelProps {
  slug: string;
  ownerName: string;
  tagline?: string;
  email?: string;
  profiles?: { network: string; url: string }[];
  greeting?: string;
  suggestions?: string[];
}
```

**(b)** Update the component signature and greeting/chips rendering. Replace the current function signature (line 47) and the JSX that renders the greeting (line 158–160) and the suggestion chips (lines 171–183) as follows.

Change the signature from:

```ts
export function ChatPanel({ slug, ownerName, tagline, email, profiles }: ChatPanelProps) {
```

to:

```ts
export function ChatPanel({ slug, ownerName, tagline, email, profiles, greeting, suggestions }: ChatPanelProps) {
```

Directly above the `return (` statement (which starts around line 144), add:

```ts
const fallbackGreeting = `Hey, I'm an agent that knows ${ownerName}.${tagline ? ` ${tagline}` : ''} Ask me anything.`;
const displayGreeting = greeting && greeting.trim() ? greeting.trim() : fallbackGreeting;
const displaySuggestions =
  Array.isArray(suggestions) && suggestions.length === 3 ? suggestions : DEFAULT_SUGGESTIONS;
```

Replace the greeting `<div>` so it reads:

```tsx
<div className="chatp-msg assistant chatp-greeting" data-testid="chat-greeting">
  <span className="chatp-prompt">&gt;</span> {displayGreeting}
</div>
```

Replace the suggestion chips block so it reads:

```tsx
{messages.length === 0 && (
  <div className="chatp-suggestions" role="group" aria-label="Suggested questions">
    {displaySuggestions.map((s) => (
      <button
        key={s}
        type="button"
        data-testid="chat-suggestion"
        className="chatp-suggestion"
        onClick={() => pickSuggestion(s)}
      >
        {s}
      </button>
    ))}
  </div>
)}
```

## Task 3: Forward `meta.agentfolio` fields from `App.tsx`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the prop-forwarding block**

In `web/src/App.tsx`, replace the `<ChatPanel …>` block (currently lines 65–72) with one that reads from `adapted.meta.agentfolio`. Locate:

```tsx
const activeSlug = slug ?? 'default';
const basics = (adapted.basics ?? {}) as IdentityBasics;
const tagline = basics.summary ? firstSentence(basics.summary) : undefined;
```

Add directly after those lines:

```tsx
const agentMeta = adapted.meta?.agentfolio;
const greeting = agentMeta?.greeting;
const suggestions = agentMeta?.suggestions;
```

Update the JSX rendering the ChatPanel (currently lines 65–72) to:

```tsx
<ChatPanel
  key={activeSlug}
  slug={activeSlug}
  ownerName={basics.name}
  tagline={tagline}
  email={basics.email}
  profiles={basics.profiles}
  greeting={greeting}
  suggestions={suggestions}
/>
```

## Task 4: Update `/fit` skill to emit greeting + suggestions

**Files:**
- Modify: `.claude/skills/fit.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the Output Format section**

In `.claude/skills/fit.md`, replace the fit-summary example (currently lines 28–41) with one that includes the new fields:

````markdown
```markdown
<!--
fit-summary:
  target: Company Name — Role Title
  changes:
    - Changed headline from "X" to "Y"
    - Reordered sections: Skills moved above Projects
    - Tailored summary for company's specific focus area
    - Emphasized relevant experience in N bullet points
    - Reordered work entries to lead with most relevant role
  greeting: Hey — I'm an agent that knows {Owner}. Ask me about the {specific project} or what drew me to {Company}.
  suggestions:
    - Why {Company}?
    - Walk me through the {specific project}
    - What's not on the résumé?
-->
# Name
...
```
````

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the description paragraph**

Below the code block, update the paragraph that describes the fields (currently line 43) from:

> The `target` field is the company and role from the JD. The `changes` list is 3-6 bullet points describing the key adaptations made. For the default fit (no JD), use `target: General` and describe which directives were applied.

to:

> The `target` field is the company and role from the JD. The `changes` list is 3-6 bullet points describing the key adaptations made. The `greeting` is a single line (1–2 sentences, ≤200 chars) written in first person as the résumé owner — it becomes the first message the chatbot says when a visitor lands on this slug. It must reference something specific from the tailored résumé (a project, stack, or company) so visitors immediately see that the chat is grounded in the adaptation. The `suggestions` list is exactly 3 opener questions, each ≤40 chars, also tailored to this slug. For the default fit (no JD), use `target: General`, describe which directives were applied, and write a generic-but-warm greeting and three generic suggestion prompts that make sense without a target company.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add .claude/skills/fit.md
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(fit): emit greeting + suggestions in fit-summary block

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Task 5: Update `/structurize` skill to write `meta.agentfolio.greeting/suggestions`

**Files:**
- Modify: `.claude/skills/structurize.md`

- [ ] **Step 1: Update the Output Schema block**

In `.claude/skills/structurize.md`, update the `meta.agentfolio` block in the schema (currently lines 72–76) to include the two new optional fields:

```json
"meta": {
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "lastModified": "<ISO 8601 timestamp>",
  "agentfolio": {
    "company": "<from slug: 'default' for default.md, company name for JD-specific>",
    "role": "<from fitted markdown title/context, or null for default>",
    "generated_by": "claude-code/structurize-skill",
    "greeting": "<greeting line from fit-summary, or omit if absent>",
    "suggestions": ["<3 items from fit-summary suggestions, or omit if absent>"]
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Update the Parsing Rules section**

Append two rules to the `## Parsing Rules` section (after the existing last rule about `meta.agentfolio.role`):

```markdown
- For `meta.agentfolio.greeting`: copy the `greeting:` value from the fit-summary block verbatim. Omit the field if the fit-summary has no `greeting:` line.
- For `meta.agentfolio.suggestions`: copy the `suggestions:` list from the fit-summary block verbatim. Omit the field if the fit-summary has no `suggestions:` list, or if the list has a number of items other than 3.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add .claude/skills/structurize.md
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(structurize): copy fit-summary greeting + suggestions into meta.agentfolio

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Task 6: Regenerate the two sample slugs and verify end-to-end

**Files (regenerated, not hand-edited):**
- `data/fitted/default.md`
- `data/fitted/notion.md`
- `data/adapted/default.json`
- `data/adapted/notion.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Rerun `/fit` for both slugs**

From the repo root, invoke the `/fit` skill. This regenerates both fitted files with the new greeting + suggestions fields in fit-summary.

Expected: `data/fitted/default.md` and `data/fitted/notion.md` each begin with a fit-summary block containing `target`, `changes`, `greeting`, and `suggestions`. Run `head -20 data/fitted/notion.md` to confirm.

- [ ] **Step 2: Rerun `/structurize` for both slugs**

Invoke the `/structurize` skill.

Expected: `data/adapted/default.json` and `data/adapted/notion.json` each contain `meta.agentfolio.greeting` (string) and `meta.agentfolio.suggestions` (array of 3). Run `jq '.meta.agentfolio' data/adapted/notion.json` to confirm.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run the full test suite**

Run: `cd web && npx vitest run`
Expected: all tests pass.

- [ ] **Step 4: Manual verify in dev server**

Run: `cd web && npm run dev` (in the background if desired). Open `/` and `/notion` (whichever the base URL is) in a browser.

Expected:
- `/` greeting matches `data/adapted/default.json` → `meta.agentfolio.greeting`. Three chips match the suggestions array.
- `/notion` greeting matches `data/adapted/notion.json` → `meta.agentfolio.greeting`. Three chips match the suggestions array.
- Typing and sending a message still streams a response through the proxy (unchanged).

- [ ] **Step 5: Negative fallback check**

Temporarily delete `meta.agentfolio.greeting` and `meta.agentfolio.suggestions` from a *local copy* of `data/adapted/default.json` (do NOT commit). Rerun `npm run copy-data` (the dev-start script runs it automatically). Reload `/`.

Expected: greeting falls back to `Hey, I'm an agent that knows {owner name}. {first sentence of summary} Ask me anything.` and chips fall back to the generic three. Restore the file from git afterwards (`git checkout data/adapted/default.json`).

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit regenerated sample data**

```bash
git add data/fitted/default.md data/fitted/notion.md data/adapted/default.json data/adapted/notion.json
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore: regenerate default + notion slugs with greeting + suggestions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Self-review checklist (run after all tasks complete)

- [ ] Spec coverage: every bullet in `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-17-llm-greeting-design.md` has a corresponding task above.
- [ ] No runtime LLM call on page load (greeting is read from static JSON).
- [ ] Offline proxy state unchanged (greeting is never rendered).
- [ ] Hand-override path works: edit fit-summary → rerun `/structurize` only → adapted JSON updates.
- [ ] Full suite + typecheck clean.
- [ ] Dashboard still renders `target`/`changes` correctly via `parseFitSummary`.
